location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday

level_signals: 10+ years of experience in product management or management consulting
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers
Compensation range $340K-$425K base, consistent with Director-level
Align stakeholders around aspirational 6-12 month roadmap

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in product management or management consulting, ideally with direct responsibility for or experience scaling B2B products and GTM systems, with a strong understanding of growth, GTM, and/or marketing pipelines.
You demonstrate a strong aptitude for and actively use data to inform your decision-making. Basic SQL proficiency is strongly preferred.

You have proven experience working and communicating effectively with senior leaders and executives, and influencing stakeholders across departments and functions, including Engineering, Design, Marketing, Sales, CRMX/Business Systems, IT, Legal, and more. You excel at building trust and working relationships.

You are a systems thinker, capable of thinking holistically across product, technical, and people systems to identify bottlenecks and opportunities for efficiency, automation, fraud prevention, and product growth within the payables ecosystem.
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers, fostering clarity, ownership, and a culture of high performance.
Define and execute a multi-quarter Growth strategy in partnership with Engineering, Design, GTM, Marketing, Ops, and Data to deliver high-impact, scalable products with measurable business impact.
You must be willing to work in the office at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Basic SQL proficiency is strongly preferred.
ideally with direct responsibility for or experience scaling B2B products and GTM systems

scope.surface: Growth Product team — customer acquisition, onboarding, GTM tooling, account scoring, TAM data, outbound automation
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — corporate cards, banking, spend management platform
core_responsibilities: Drive product and systems investments that improve GTM metrics such as CAC payback, pipeline conversion, and efficiency.
Define and execute a multi-quarter Growth strategy in partnership with Engineering, Design, GTM, Marketing, Ops, and Data to deliver high-impact, scalable products with measurable business impact.
Embed within Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to identify workflow bottlenecks and lead automation and optimization initiatives.
Analyze GTM funnel and conversion metrics to uncover insights and guide product priorities and roadmap decisions.
Set clear goals, metrics, and success criteria for the Growth team, driving accountability to measurable outcomes.
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers, fostering clarity, ownership, and a culture of high performance.
Oversee core Growth Product and Engineering domains, including account scoring, TAM data, outbound automation, and GTM tooling.
Align stakeholders around an aspirational 6–12 month roadmap that advances GTM efficiency and business growth.
Ensure successful activation and implementation of customers initiatives by partnering with GTM and Customer Success to operationalize tooling and automations.
Continuously refine activation based on feedback and performance data to drive faster time-to-value and sustainable adoption.

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and highly competitive ($340K-$425K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Growth Product team with specific domains named)
Concrete examples of past work shared (AI agents for GTM, ML account prioritization, prospect AI classification)
Well-defined hybrid policy with specific days stated (Mon/Wed/Thu)
Equity mentioned as part of total comp package
red_flags: Strict in-office requirement 3 days/week (Mon, Wed, Thu) in SF, Seattle, or NYC — eliminates remote candidates
Very broad role spanning AI, growth, GTM tooling, and people management simultaneously — scope may be overwhelming
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
10+ year requirement with Director-level expectations is a high bar that may limit candidate pool

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years requirement, people management, and Director title)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed directly in JD)
location.pay_differential (inferred from JD statement 'starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate's location')
ambiguities: SQL proficiency is described as 'strongly preferred' under the Analytical sense requirement, which sits in the Requirements section — treated as nice-to-have (soft) rather than hard given the 'preferred' qualifier, though its placement in Requirements could imply hard. Flagged here for review.
Role title includes 'AI' but the JD body emphasizes growth/GTM tooling and AI agents for GTM workflows — archetype is Growth PM with AI secondary, though AI Product Manager could also apply.
Remote eligibility: JD states employees can work remotely 'up to 4 weeks per year' suggesting the role is not remote-eligible beyond this limited allowance — treated as hybrid/onsite.
